title: Boss Encounters
problem: Every 5 floors the player deserves a climactic fight — currently all encounters draw from the same random pool with no milestone moments. There are no named bosses, no special mechanics (enrage, phase shifts, immunity windows), and no guaranteed high-quality loot rewards. The dungeon has no sense of pacing or act structure.
analysis: EncounterType (engine/encounter.ts) has Normal, Elite, Rare, Ancient, Chest — no Boss. GridScreen generates random encounters with no floor milestone logic. monsters.ts has generic monster templates but no named boss instances. combatStore.handleVictory awards XP/loot uniformly. Two gaps: (1) no boss spawning logic tied to floor milestones, (2) no special mechanic layer in applyCombatAction for boss phases like enrage or regeneration.
implementation: 1. src/data/bosses.ts — BossDef interface: { id, name, floorRequired, hpMultiplier, dmgMultiplier, xpMultiplier, mechanics: BossMechanic[] }. BossMechanic = 'enrage' | 'regen' | 'immune_round' | 'no_flee'. Define 6 bosses: The Warden (fl5), Bonekeeper (fl10), Inferno Witch (fl15), Shadow Stalker (fl20), Iron Colossus (fl25), The Abyssal One (fl30+, repeating every 10 after). 2. engine/encounter.ts — add EncounterType.Boss. Add isBossFloor(floor): boolean = floor > 0 && floor % 5 === 0. Add spawnBoss(floor, rng): MonsterInstance — picks boss by floorRequired, applies hpMultiplier/dmgMultiplier. 3. engine/combat.ts — CombatAction gains bossEnraged?: boolean. applyCombatAction checks round > 5 && boss → apply 1.5× dmg multiplier. Immune round: every 7th round boss takes 0 damage. Regen: boss heals 3% maxHp each round. 4. store/gridStore.ts — when generating encounter for a tile, if isBossFloor(floor) and no boss yet killed this floor, force EncounterType.Boss. Store bossKilledOnFloor: number[] to prevent respawn. 5. store/combatStore.ts — fleeAction checks encounterType === Boss → log 'Cannot flee from boss!' and return. Boss victory awards 3× XP. 6. engine/loot.ts — bossLootTable: guaranteed rare + 30% unique + 3–5 item drops total. 7. CombatScreen.tsx — boss header: red pulsing name with BOSS badge, phase indicator ('ENRAGED' after round 5), flee button disabled with tooltip.

decisions.decision: Boss spawns every 5 floors, not randomly
decisions.rationale: Predictable milestones let players prepare (use town portal, equip best gear). Random boss floors would feel punishing with no agency.
alternativesConsidered: Random boss among floors 3-7, 8-12 etc — less predictable, less satisfying

decisions.decision: Enrage triggers at round 5, not a HP threshold
decisions.rationale: Round-based enrage punishes turtling (using potions indefinitely) without being unfair to low-damage builds. HP threshold enrage is invisible and frustrating.
alternativesConsidered: 50% HP enrage — invisible to player until it happens
No enrage — boss is just a big HP bar

decisions.rationale: Boss is a commitment. Fleeing would trivialize the milestone. Players who aren't ready should use a town portal scroll before entering the boss room.
alternativesConsidered: Allow flee with heavy penalty — dilutes the stakes

successCriteria: Floor 5 always spawns a boss encounter. Boss cannot be fled. Boss enrages at round 5 (1.5× damage). Boss drops 3+ items with at least 1 rare. Each named boss has a unique display name and different mechanic combination. Boss kills grant 3× XP.
knownLimitations: Boss roster is 6 named bosses — floors 35+ repeat The Abyssal One variant
No boss cutscene or intro animation in v1
Immune round mechanic is not visually telegraphed — just 0 damage in log

bossDefeated is run-scoped (floor-level, in gridStore) and not persisted — if the app crashes after defeating a boss but before calling nextFloor, the boss will not re-spawn (bossDefeated stays true in memory until the next initFloor call)
componentFile: src/data/bosses.ts, src/engine/encounter.ts, src/engine/combat.ts, src/store/gridStore.ts, src/store/combatStore.ts, src/screens/CombatScreen.tsx
userGuide:
## Boss Encounters

Every 5 floors you'll face a named boss — a far more dangerous fight than anything else on that floor.

**How to find the boss:** The boss room appears somewhere on the floor grid. It looks like a normal tile but the encounter is forced — you can't skip it on a boss floor.

**You cannot flee from a boss.** Come prepared: use a Town Portal Scroll to manage your gear before floor 5, 10, 15...

**Enrage:** After round 5 the boss enters an enraged state — its damage increases by 50%. Fast fights are rewarded. Healing through infinite rounds will get you killed.

**Rewards:** Boss kills guarantee at least one rare item and several other drops. They also give 3× the XP of a normal monster — the fastest way to level.

codexEntry.title: Boss Encounters
codexEntry.summary: Named boss monsters appear every 5 floors. They are mandatory, cannot be fled, and become enraged after round 5.
mechanics: Boss spawns on every floor that is a multiple of 5
Enrage at round 5: boss deals 1.5× damage
Some bosses regenerate HP each round
Some bosses have a 1-round immunity window (0 damage received)
Boss kills: 3× XP, guaranteed rare drop, 30% chance for unique
tips: Always visit town before a boss floor if you have a scroll.
Use your highest-damage skills in rounds 1-4 before enrage.
Mana Shield (Sorcerer) is exceptionally strong against boss enrage bursts.

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Initial full feature definition for boss encounters

publicInterface.name: EncounterType.Boss
publicInterface.description: New encounter type for boss fights

publicInterface.name: isBossFloor
publicInterface.description: Returns true when floor is a multiple of 5

publicInterface.name: BossDef
publicInterface.description: Named boss definition with mechanics flags and stat multipliers

publicInterface.name: spawnBoss
publicInterface.description: Generates a boss MonsterInstance scaled to current floor

codeSnippets.label: Boss enrage logic in applyCombatAction
codeSnippets.snippet:
// In applyCombatAction, before damage calc:
const isEnraged = isBoss && round > 5
const monsterDmgMult = isEnraged ? 1.5 : 1.0
const monsterRawDmg = roll(rng, monster.damage[0], monster.damage[1]) * monsterDmgMult

codeSnippets.label: Flee guard in combatStore.fleeAction
